skill.name: sentry-automation
skill.description: Automate Sentry issue triage, alert setup, release tracking, monitor updates, and organization review through Rube MCP and Composio's Sentry toolkit.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to operate common Sentry workflows from an AI assistant.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code spans and Sentry tool names, not executable Ruby or shell code. The skill does require an external Rube MCP endpoint and can read or modify Sentry data after OAuth, so it carries medium operational and data-access risk.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Sentry Data and Configuration Access
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to use Rube MCP with an active Sentry connection, then list issues, read events, create alert rules, manage releases, upload release files, update monitors, and list organization members. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it can expose sensitive operational data or change monitoring behavior if used with broad Sentry permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly document authenticated Sentry workflows with read and write capabilities. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is permission scope and misuse rather than confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many backtick-delimited strings as Ruby shell execution, but the file is Markdown documentation and the strings are tool names, parameters, and examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, eval pattern, or command interpolation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is Markdown guidance and contains no executable code blocks that run shell commands. The repeated matches align with inline code formatting around Sentry tool and parameter names.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded MCP Endpoint Is a Declared Service Dependency
low_findings.description: The skill includes the Rube MCP server URL as setup documentation. This is a true network dependency, but it is not hidden exfiltration because the endpoint is disclosed and tied to the required MCP integration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 20 directly documents an external MCP endpoint. The context is setup instructions for the named service rather than covert network activity.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Static Findings Are Not Supported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding on the description line and the reconnaissance findings around schema, detail, and event references are not supported by the Markdown content. No cryptographic code, host discovery, system inventory, or local environment probing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss Sentry schemas, issue details, and event details. They do not contain cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance commands.

content.user_title: Automate Sentry Operations with Rube MCP
content.value_statement: Sentry teams often spend time switching between issues, alerts, releases, projects, and organization settings. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for using Rube MCP to perform common Sentry operations.
actual_capabilities: Lists and inspects Sentry organization issues, issue details, events, and tag distributions.
Finds project slugs and retrieves project-scoped issue lists.
Creates and updates Sentry alert rules using Rube MCP tool schemas.
Creates releases, updates release metadata, records deploys, and uploads release files.
Lists organization teams, members, projects, and monitor configuration details.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP to be configured in the user's AI client.
Requires an active authenticated Sentry connection through Rube MCP.
Available actions depend on the connected Sentry account permissions.
Tool schemas may change, so the skill instructs the assistant to search tools first.

use_cases.title: Triage Production Errors
use_cases.description: Review unresolved issues, inspect events, compare tags, and identify likely impact before assigning follow-up work.

use_cases.title: Maintain Alert Rules
use_cases.description: List existing rules, create project alerts, update metric alerts, and verify rule details before rollout.

use_cases.title: Track Releases and Deploys
use_cases.description: Create releases, associate projects, record deployments, and review issues tied to release versions.

prompt_templates.title: List Open Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Sentry automation skill to list unresolved issues for my organization. Search Rube tools first, then ask for any missing organization or project identifiers.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate an Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate Sentry issue [issue id]. Retrieve issue details, recent events, stack trace context, and tag distribution. Summarize likely impact and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Alert
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Sentry alert rule for project [project]. Search current schemas first, confirm conditions and actions with me, then create the rule.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Release Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up monitoring for release [version]. Create or update the release, record the deploy, check related unresolved issues, and report any risks.

output_examples.input: Show unresolved Sentry issues for the payments project.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the project slug, lists unresolved issues, groups them by priority and frequency, and highlights issues needing immediate review.

output_examples.input: Create an alert when checkout errors spike.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches current alert schemas, confirms the project, trigger threshold, notification action, and frequency, then creates the alert rule.
The assistant creates or updates the release, records the production deploy, checks release-related issues, and summarizes monitoring gaps.

best_practices: Use least-privilege Sentry permissions for the connected account.
Review alert rule changes before allowing the assistant to create or update them.
Ask the assistant to summarize planned Sentry mutations before execution.
anti_patterns: Do not connect an unrestricted administrator account for routine triage.
Do not create noisy alert rules without checking frequency and trigger conditions.
Do not paste secrets or private customer data into prompts.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Sentry API key?
faq.answer: The skill expects authentication through Rube MCP and Composio. It does not instruct users to paste API keys into prompts.

faq.question: Can it change Sentry settings?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents workflows for creating alert rules, updating alerts, updating monitors, and managing releases.

faq.question: Why does it search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameters.

faq.question: Is it safe for read-only triage?
faq.answer: It can support read-only triage if the connected Sentry account has limited permissions.

faq.question: What data can it access?
faq.answer: It can access Sentry data permitted by the connected account, including issues, events, teams, projects, releases, and monitors.

faq.question: Does it run local commands?
faq.answer: No local command execution was found in the skill file. The backtick detections were Markdown formatting.
